title: Apache Tomcat — known default credentials
description: Apache Tomcat ships with default accounts (e.g. tomcat/tomcat, admin/admin) on /manager/html. Verify they are removed or rotated.
remediation: Remove or rename the default tomcat-users.xml accounts and restrict /manager/html to trusted networks.

title: Jenkins — known default credentials
description: Older Jenkins installs allow admin/admin or anonymous setup. Confirm initial setup wizard has been completed and unauthenticated access is disabled.
remediation: Disable anonymous read, rotate the admin password, and enable matrix-based auth.

title: Grafana — default admin/admin credentials
description: Grafana ships with admin/admin. Forced password change is opt-in and frequently skipped.
remediation: Rotate the admin password and force_change_password=true in grafana.ini.

title: phpMyAdmin — common default DB credentials
description: phpMyAdmin frequently exposes a login page where the underlying MySQL still uses root with no password.
remediation: Restrict phpMyAdmin to localhost or VPN; ensure DB users have strong passwords.

title: MongoDB — auth disabled by default
description: MongoDB historically launches with --noauth. Open 27017 with no auth implies unauthenticated read/write.
remediation: Enable authentication (security.authorization=enabled) and bind to private interfaces only.

title: Redis — no default password
description: Redis runs without authentication unless requirepass is set. Exposed 6379 implies unauthenticated access.
remediation: Set requirepass, bind to localhost, or restrict via firewall.

title: Kibana — anonymous access likely
description: Kibana frequently runs with no authentication when the X-Pack security plugin is disabled.
remediation: Enable X-Pack security and require login on Kibana endpoints.

title: Elasticsearch — no auth by default
description: Elasticsearch versions before 8.0 do not enable security by default. Exposed 9200 implies unauthenticated cluster access.
remediation: Upgrade to 8.x with built-in security, or enable X-Pack security and TLS.
